source: Curated per-vendor VoIP DHCP-option roster (issue #112). Each vendor entry lists the DHCP options that vendor's phones look for when bootstrapping their TFTP / HTTP provisioning server. The match_hint is the DHCP option-60 (vendor-class-id) substring that operators most commonly use to fence the matching client-class.

vendors.description: Polycom VVX / SoundPoint / SoundStation phones. Modern firmware reads option 160 first; older firmware falls back to option 66.

options.name: polycom-config-url
options.use: Provisioning URL — recommended over option 66 for VVX firmware.

vendors.description: Yealink T-series phones (T19/T2x/T4x/T5x). Reads option 66 + option 161.
Cisco SPA / Linksys SPA series (SPA303/SPA50x/SPA52x). Reads option 66 + option 150 + option 132.

options.name: tftp-server-address
options.use: TFTP server IP list (RFC 5859) — Cisco preferred over option 66.

vendors.description: Mitel phones (Aastra heritage). Uses option 43 vendor-specific sub-options.

options.name: vendor-encapsulated-options
options.use: Vendor sub-option bundle — encode call server / TFTP / image URL per Mitel docs.

vendors.description: Avaya 9600 / J-series phones. Modern firmware reads option 242; legacy firmware reads option 176.
Snom phones. Reads option 66 for the provisioning server hostname.

options.name: tftp-server-name
options.use: Provisioning server hostname (HTTP / HTTPS / TFTP per Snom URL syntax).

vendors.description: Grandstream GXP / GXV phones. Reads option 66 for the provisioning server.
Aastra phones (now Mitel — kept separate for older firmware that still identifies as Aastra).
